author: Yokes
text:
lifecycle: state-branch sync on review and close

bl-2148 made the claim transition observably atomic via the bl-eae4
negotiation primitive: the local claim commit on `balls/tasks` only
counts after origin accepts it. bl-2bf7 generalizes the same wire to
review and close, completing the SPEC §6 set of state-branch lifecycle
events that subscribe to the git-remote participant.

`require_remote_on_review` and `require_remote_on_close` config fields
mirror `_on_claim` with the same precedence chain: CLI > local clone
> repo default. New `bl review --sync` / `--no-sync` and
`bl close --sync` / `--no-sync` flags surface the per-invocation knob.
A required-policy push failure rolls back the local commits — review
restores main and the state-branch tip; close restores the state
branch before tearing down the worktree, so a rejected close keeps
the worktree intact for retry.

The `GitRemoteClaimProtocol` gains an `event` field; only the claim
event runs the post-merge ownership check, since review and close
have no "lost" outcome. `GitRemoteParticipant::for_lifecycle(events)`
joins `for_claim()` for callers that opt into review/close.
